title: Zoom API JWT secrets should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Zoom JWT token detected. JWT (JSON Web Token) tokens are used for authenticating Zoom API requests including meeting management, user administration, webinars, and recordings. Exposing JWT tokens allows attackers to impersonate your application, create/modify meetings, access user data, manage webinars, and perform unauthorized API operations. Immediately rotate your JWT credentials in the Zoom Marketplace and audit all API access logs for suspicious activity.

Zoom JWT token detected in Authorization header. Bearer tokens in authorization headers provide direct authenticated access to Zoom API endpoints for meeting management, user operations, webinar controls, and recording access. Exposing authorization headers with JWT tokens enables attackers to fully impersonate your application and perform any API operations your credentials allow. Immediately revoke and regenerate your JWT credentials from the Zoom Marketplace and review all recent API activity.

Zoom API secret detected. API secrets are used with API keys to generate JWT tokens for Zoom API authentication. Exposing API secrets allows attackers to generate valid JWT tokens, enabling full access to your Zoom account's API capabilities including meeting creation, user management, webinar controls, and recording access. Immediately regenerate your API secret in the Zoom Marketplace app credentials section and update all applications using the old secret.
